meaning: adj.热锅上的蚂蚁般的,坐立不安的
examples: This is the end of a tour so I'm a little antsy, I guess.
Personally, I get antsy when I think I'm boring someone else.
It's a happy moment, even if the lack of Wi-Fi makes Levchin antsy.
If not, the whole ratification by conduct thing and quantum meruit considerations get quite antsy.
They got impatient, antsy, wanted someone to go out and see what was happening.
